# Retire flat era-owned facts from identityRoots: migrate the 8 consumer read-paths onto the hydration index

## Context

The retirement half split from #14731 at implementation (consumer-census-driven): the migration module now expresses every live agent resident through the identity schema (seed eras carry the flat facts verbatim; THE PROPERTY proven on production data), but the flat era-owned fields cannot leave `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` until every consumer's read path moves.

## The consumer census (V-B-A'd 2026-07-04)

`modelFamily`/`family` are load-bearing in: `ai/services/memory-core/MailboxService.mjs` (A2A `AGENT:<family>/<model>` alias resolution â€” ambiguity rejection reads `properties.modelFamily`), `ai/services/memory-core/WakeSubscriptionService.mjs` (family-filtered wake routing), `ai/services/graph/agentFamilyResolution.mjs` (the resolver itself). Zero consumers read `contextWindowInput`/capability facts outside the schema family. 8+ modules import `identityRoots` for identity-level operational fields (trust tiers, wake routes, mailbox addresses) â€” those stay identity-level by design and are NOT part of this retirement.

## The Fix

- Each family/model read-path consumes `buildHydrationIndex(...).index.currentEra.family` (with `isIndexCurrent` gating any cached read) instead of the flat property â€” one consumer per commit, regression per consumer (alias resolution and wake routing must behave identically before/after).
- Then delete the era-owned flat fields (`modelFamily` duplicates, `contextWindowInput`, `tier`, `hosting`, `parallelToolCalls`, `thoughtBudget`, `sunsetTriggers`) from the registry entries â€” the identity keeps only the anchor, social layer, and identity-level lifecycle/operational fields.
- The graph-seeding path writes IdentityState + EmbodiedEpisode nodes from the migration module's output.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Zero era-owned facts flat on `identityRoots.mjs` entries.
- [ ] A2A alias resolution + wake family-routing regressions green before AND after each consumer move.
- [ ] Cached identity reads gated by `isIndexCurrent`.
- [ ] The reflexive-landing fixture â†” production agreement AC (carried from #14731) lands here, meaningful once bearer-audited era backfill exists.

PR chain #14729 â†’ #14730 â†’ the #14731 migration PR (this consumes all three).

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-04T22:48:47Z

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Intake verdict: **valid in-scope transition, not code-ready until the routing contract is explicit**. I applied `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` so this does not remain claimable before the Contract Ledger exists.

V-B-A evidence checked before classification:
- Live issue body/comments: no comments, no assignee, no closing PRs, and no Contract Ledger matrix.
- Parent #14677 is an epic and already has @neo-gpt epic-review. Current parent status says #14750 is the remaining in-scope flatâ†’era transition leaf.
- Prerequisite PRs are merged: #14729 schema/reflexive fixture, #14730 hydration, #14751 migration, and #14791 render-contract.
- Current source still matches the ticket's risk premise: `identityRoots.mjs` still has flat era-owned facts (`modelFamily`, `contextWindowInput`, `hosting`, `tier`, `parallelToolCalls`, `thoughtBudget`, `sunsetTriggers`, etc.); `MailboxService.validateMailboxTarget()` still resolves `AGENT:<family>/<model>` aliases through `props.modelFamily`; `WakeSubscriptionService._listAgentIdentityNodes()` still filters `props.family || props.modelFamily`; `agentFamilyResolution.getCoreSwarmAgentFamilies()` still derives review-family maps from `identityRoots` flat `modelFamily`.
- `identityHydration.mjs` provides the intended replacement primitive (`buildHydrationIndex`, `isIndexCurrent`, `index.currentEra.family`); `identityRootsMigration.mjs` already proves the seed-era projection on production data.
- Successor/duplicate sweep found only #14750 as the open issue and only the prerequisite merged PRs, not an implementation PR that closes this retirement.
- ADR successor-risk: `adr-aligned` â€” the work consumes ADR-0032/#14677 era-shape authority rather than challenging it.

Why this blocks branch work: this is not just a private refactor. It changes consumed routing behavior for A2A addressing, wake family filters, and cross-family review/family resolution. Per `learn/agentos/process/contract-ledger.md`, consumed runtime/agent surfaces need a T3 Contract Ledger before implementation so edge cases do not get invented in review.

Please update the body with a Contract Ledger covering at least:

| Target Surface | Contract detail needed before implementation |
|---|---|
| `MailboxService` `AGENT:<family>/<model>` alias resolution | Source of family truth after migration; ambiguity/zero-match behavior; how hydration index staleness is handled; exact regression tests for canonical and ambiguous aliases |
| `WakeSubscriptionService` family filtering / liveness projection | Source of family truth; fallback when no valid/current era exists; parity test for family-filtered online routing |
| `agentFamilyResolution` / cross-family review maps | How GitHub login â†’ family map reads the current era; behavior for non-agent/system identities; parity tests for review-family coverage |
| `identityRoots.mjs` flat-field retirement | Exact field list to remove vs identity-level fields to retain; any JSDoc/comment updates; proof that trust-tier/wake-route/mailbox-address fields stay identity-level |
| Hydration index cache policy | Where indexes are built/cached, what `isIndexCurrent` gates, and what consumers do on stale/missing/invalid chains |
| Graph-seeding path | Which code writes IdentityState + EmbodiedEpisode nodes from `identityRootsMigration.mjs`; idempotence and failure behavior |
| Evidence sequencing | Per-consumer tests before/after each move, plus final `rg` proving zero era-owned flat facts remain |

Current classification: `needs-narrowing` / `needs-contract-alignment` via available labels `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`. The goal is current and important; the missing piece is the routing contract.
